headline: Competitive Positioning Memo: 5 Agency Pain Points + Defensible Moat Analysis
highlights: Critical pain point: Agencies spend 6-10 hours/week on manual reporting = $150-250M beachhead labor waste
Competitor analysis: Supermetrics ($168+/user), Whatagraph ($249+), Looker ($2K+) all overpriced for 10-50 person shops
5 unmet needs validated: time automation, integration simplicity, SMB pricing ($100/user target), real-time dashboards, agency workflow templates
Defensible moat: Agency-first design (5/5), pricing moat (4/5), speed-to-value (5/5), beachhead lock-in (4/5), brand loyalty (4/5)
Positioning: 'Automated Marketing Reporting Built for Digital Agencies' — 30-minute setup vs 4-6 week competitor average
Decision factors: ReportEase wins on speed (30 min), cost ($1K/10 users), dashboard quality, setup simplicity, multi-client scaling, white-labeling
Validation metrics: 6x faster setup, 33% cheaper, 95% automation vs 60% competitors, NPS 50+ vs 30 competitor average

next_actions.label: Validate pricing assumption via survey
next_actions.task_template: Execute pricing sensitivity survey with 10-15 agencies on willingness-to-pay at $100, $150, $200/user/month

next_actions.label: Test dashboard design with agencies
next_actions.task_template: Show 5-10 agencies mockups of ReportEase dashboards vs Supermetrics/Whatagraph; measure preference and satisfaction

next_actions.label: Define agency template workflows
next_actions.task_template: Interview 3-5 agencies on top 3 client profiles; define and test template-based setup workflows
